ingredients.name: Hikarigaoka Station
ingredients.localName: 光が丘駅
ingredients.address: Hikarigaoka Station, 3-chōme-10 Hikarigaoka, Nerima City, Tokyo 179-0072, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hikarigaoka in Nerima City is the neighborhood where the DigiDestined grew up, and its surroundings appear in flashback sequences that establish the children's lives before their adventure in the Digital World. The station and its distinctive shopping complex anchor the series' hometown setting.

ingredients.name: Yagura Bridge
ingredients.localName: やぐら橋
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge in the Odaiba waterfront district appears during the Myotismon arc (episode 35) as the DigiDestined chase Myotismon's minions through Tokyo's bayside area. The urban canal setting contrasts sharply with the Digital World backdrops fans had seen throughout the series.

ingredients.name: PRONTO - Tokyo Big Sight
ingredients.localName: PRONTO 東京ビッグサイト店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒135-0063 Tokyo, Koto City, Ariake, 3 Chome−11−1 東京ビッグサイト PRONTO - Tokyo Big Sight, 国際展示場 会議棟
ingredients.entityContext: Located near Tokyo Big Sight in Ariake, this cafe area appears in episode 35 during the Myotismon invasion of the real world, when the DigiDestined and their partners pursue Demidevimon's forces through the bayside convention district.

ingredients.name: Fuji Television Network, Inc.
ingredients.localName: (株)フジテレビジョン
ingredients.address: Fuji Television Network, Inc., 2 Chome-4-8 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 137-8088, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Fuji TV headquarters on Odaiba serves as a backdrop during the Myotismon arc (episode 37), when the DigiDestined battle Myotismon's forces across the man-made island. The iconic spherical building is among the most recognizable real-world landmarks to appear in the series.

ingredients.name: Hinode Pier
ingredients.localName: 日の出客船ターミナル
ingredients.address: Hinode Pier, 2 Chome-7-104 Kaigan, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hinode Pier appears in episode 35 during the Myotismon invasion arc, as the DigiDestined and their Digimon partners move through Tokyo's waterfront in pursuit of Myotismon. The pier's ferry terminal setting underscores the series' use of real Tokyo geography during the real-world arc.

ingredients.name: Saw, Sawing
ingredients.localName: 「Saw, Sawing (切っている鋸) 」クレス・オルデンバーグ＆コーシャ・ヴァン・ブリュッゲン
ingredients.address: Saw, Sawing, 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The large Claes Oldenburg sculpture outside Tokyo Big Sight appears in episode 35 as a background landmark during the Myotismon arc, when the DigiDestined battle through the Odaiba convention area. Pilgrims recognize the oversized saw sculpture from scene comparisons in the pilgrimage community.

ingredients.name: Playground equipment Square
ingredients.localName: 遊具広場
ingredients.entityContext: This small playground near Odaiba appears in episode 35 during the Myotismon invasion, serving as a location where the children and their Digimon partners regroup or pass through during the chase across the bayside district.

ingredients.name: Fuji Television Headquarters “HACHITAMA” Spherical Observation Room
ingredients.localName: フジテレビ球体展望室 はちたま
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒135-0091 Tokyo, Minato City, Daiba, 2 Chome−4−8 Fuji Television Headquarters “HACHITAMA” Spherical Observation Room, フジ テレビ 25 階
ingredients.entityContext: The Hachitama sphere atop Fuji TV's Odaiba headquarters is a visually iconic landmark featured in the Myotismon arc (episode 35), appearing in background shots as the DigiDestined battle across Odaiba. Fans visit to photograph the sphere against the Tokyo skyline just as it appeared in the show.

ingredients.name: Hands Shibuya
ingredients.localName: ハンズ渋谷店
ingredients.address: 12-18 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyu Hands (now Hands) in Shibuya appears in episode 33 during the Myotismon arc when Myotismon's servants Pumpkinmon and Gotsumon roam Shibuya causing chaos. The familiar retail storefront is visible as the children chase the two wayward Digimon through the shopping district.

ingredients.name: Shibuya 109
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-29-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The landmark Shibuya 109 shopping tower appears in episode 33 as Myotismon's minions Pumpkinmon and Gotsumon rampage through Shibuya, with the DigiDestined pursuing them past this iconic fashion hub. The 109 building is one of the most clearly recognizable real-world locations in the episode.

ingredients.name: Shinjuku Station Odakyu Line South Exit Ticket
ingredients.localName: 新宿駅小田急線南口改札
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Odakyu Line south exit of Shinjuku Station appears in episode 30 during the Myotismon arc, when the DigiDestined are tracking leads on the eighth child's location through Tokyo. The busy commuter gate captures the tension of the children moving covertly through the city.

ingredients.name: Spain-zaka Slope
ingredients.localName: スペイン坂
ingredients.address: 16-15 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Spain-zaka, the stepped pedestrian lane in Shibuya's Udagawacho district, appears in episode 33 as Pumpkinmon and Gotsumon wander Shibuya and the DigiDestined follow. The atmospheric alley setting is a documented pilgrimage spot for fans of the Shibuya episodes.

ingredients.name: Yashirobashi
ingredients.localName: 社橋
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-17 Shinbashi, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yashirobashi bridge appears in episode 35 during the Myotismon arc battle in the Odaiba and waterfront area, serving as a transit point as the DigiDestined and their partners sweep through bayside Tokyo pursuing Myotismon's forces.

ingredients.name: Odaiba-Kaihin-Koen Station.
ingredients.localName: お台場海浜公園駅
ingredients.address: Odaiba-kaihinkōen Station, 2-chōme-3 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Odaiba-Kaihin-Koen Station on the Yurikamome line appears in episode 21 when the DigiDestined briefly return to the real world and navigate the Odaiba waterfront. The elevated monorail station above Tokyo Bay is a memorable landmark from the real-world arc.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Odaiba Kaihin Koen Store
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート お台場東京ビーチ店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart, 1 Chome-6-1 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This FamilyMart convenience store near Odaiba beach park appears in episode 21, when the DigiDestined return to the real world and pass through the Daiba district. Convenience store sightings are among the more granular pilgrimage details that dedicated fans track.

ingredients.name: Daiba Children’s Hall
ingredients.localName: 港区 台場児童館
ingredients.address: Daiba Children’s Hall, １Ｆ 1 Chome-5-1 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Daiba Children's Hall appears in episode 21 during the DigiDestined's brief return to Tokyo, set in the residential Daiba district. The community facility reflects the series' attention to everyday Tokyo locations that would resonate with Japanese viewers.

ingredients.name: Daiba Civic Center
ingredients.localName: 港区立台場区民センター
ingredients.address: Daiba Civic Center, 3階 1 Chome-5-1 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Daiba Civic Center appears in episode 21 as part of the Daiba district backdrop when the DigiDestined briefly return to the real world. Its mundane civic setting grounds the fantastical story in recognizable Tokyo geography.

ingredients.name: 25 porticos
ingredients.localName: 「25のポルティコ―色彩と反映」ダニエル・ビュレンス
ingredients.entityContext: The Daniel Buren installation of colored porticos near the Daiba seaside area appears in episode 21, visible as the DigiDestined navigate the Odaiba waterfront during their return to the real world. The distinctive striped columns make it a recognizable scene-matching spot for pilgrims.

ingredients.name: Lawson H Odaiba Kaihin Koen-mae store
ingredients.localName: ローソン Ｈお台場海浜公園前店
ingredients.address: Lawson, 1 Chome-3-5 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Lawson convenience store near Odaiba Beach Park appears in episode 21 during the DigiDestined's brief real-world interlude in the Daiba district. Like other retail locations in the arc, it reflects the production team's detailed recreation of Odaiba's streetscape.

ingredients.name: Tsukimio Bridge
ingredients.localName: 月見大橋
ingredients.address: Tsukimio Bridge, 3 Chome-10 Hikarigaoka, Nerima City, Tokyo 179-0072, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsukimio Bridge in Hikarigaoka (Nerima) appears in episode 29 as part of the Hikarigaoka hometown setting, when the Myotismon arc brings the action back to the neighborhood where the DigiDestined grew up. The bridge is steps from Hikarigaoka Station and appears in pilgrimage guides alongside the station.

ingredients.name: Starbucks Coffee - Hikarigaoka IMA
ingredients.localName: スターバックス コーヒー 光が丘IMA店
ingredients.address: Starbucks Coffee - Hikarigaoka IMA, Ima, 5 Chome-1-1 Hikarigaoka, Nerima City, Tokyo 179-0072, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Hikarigaoka IMA shopping center Starbucks appears in episode 30 during scenes set in the DigiDestined's home neighborhood of Hikarigaoka. The IMA mall is part of the same complex that defines the Hikarigaoka town center and recurs throughout the real-world arc.

ingredients.name: Wendy’s First Kitchen - Shinjuku South Entrance
ingredients.localName: ウェンディーズ・ファーストキッチン 新宿南口店
ingredients.address: Wendy’s First Kitchen - Shinjuku South Entrance, 村上ビル 1F 1 Chome-18-3 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Wendy's First Kitchen near Shinjuku Station's south entrance appears in episode 30 during the Myotismon arc when the DigiDestined track the eighth child through Shinjuku. The fast-food location is a documented scene-matching spot from the arc's Shinjuku episodes.

ingredients.name: LUMINE Shinjuku 1
ingredients.localName: ルミネ新宿 ルミネ1
ingredients.address: LUMINE Shinjuku 1, 1 Chome-1-5 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: LUMINE 1, the shopping complex directly connected to Shinjuku Station, appears in episode 30 as the DigiDestined search the south exit area of Shinjuku for clues about the eighth child during the Myotismon arc. The building's south-exit facade is a clear scene match for pilgrims.

ingredients.name: YAMADA Denki LABI Shinjuku Nishiguchi
ingredients.localName: ヤマダデンキ LABI新宿西口館
ingredients.address: YAMADA Denki LABI Shinjuku Nishiguchi, 1 Chome-18-8 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Yamada Denki electronics store near Shinjuku's west exit appears in episode 30 during the Myotismon arc, as the DigiDestined sweep through the Shinjuku south and west areas searching for the eighth DigiDestined child. The storefront is a documented location match in pilgrimage records.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Tower
ingredients.localName: 東京タワー
ingredients.address: Tokyo Tower, 4 Chome-2-8 Shibakoen, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0011
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Tower appears in episode 32 during the Myotismon arc when Myotismon's invasion of Tokyo intensifies, with the tower's red-and-white silhouette appearing against the night sky as the DigiDestined grapple with Myotismon's growing power. Tokyo Tower is the most iconic real-world landmark to appear in the series.

ingredients.name: MEGA Don Quijote
ingredients.localName: MEGAドン・キホーテ 渋谷本店
ingredients.address: MEGA Don Quijote, 28-6 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Don Quijote discount store in Shibuya's Udagawacho appears in episode 33 during the Pumpkinmon and Gotsumon rampage through Shibuya, visible as one of the recognizable storefront landmarks in the shopping district scenes.

ingredients.name: Daiba Park
ingredients.localName: 台場公園
ingredients.address: Daiba Park, 1 Chome-10-1 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091
ingredients.entityContext: Daiba Park, the historic fort-island park in Tokyo Bay, appears in episode 34 during the Myotismon arc as the DigiDestined battle through the Odaiba area. The park's stone fortifications and bay views give it a distinctive look that fans match against episode backgrounds.
